title: Star Gazing Nights
description: Monthly public observation events on campus where members set up telescopes and guide attendees through the night sky.

title: Astrophotography Workshops
description: Hands-on sessions teaching members how to photograph planets, nebulae, and star fields using club equipment.

title: Telescope Training
description: One-on-one or small group sessions teaching beginners how to set up, align, and operate a telescope.

title: Guest Speaker Events
description: Talks hosted by astronomy professors, NASA researchers, or local planetarium staff on topics like space exploration and cosmology.

title: Dark Sky Road Trips
description: Organized trips away from city light pollution to remote locations for deep-sky observation.

title: Planetary Imaging Sessions
description: Dedicated club nights focused on imaging planets like Jupiter, Saturn, and Mars during favorable viewing windows.

title: Intro to Astronomy Seminar
description: A beginner-friendly crash course covering constellations, the solar system, and how to navigate the night sky.

title: Astrophotography Print Sales
description: Professionally printed photos of deep-sky objects and planets taken by club members, available to support club funding.
